Walk Sharper.
Think Faster.
Age Better.

StrideMind pairs cognitive challenges with your daily walk — clinically‑backed dual‑task training designed for adults 55+.

Download on theApp Store
✓Free to start✓No equipment needed✓iOS app

How It Works

Three steps to sharper thinking

🎧

Step 01

Put in your earbuds

Grab your regular earbuds or headphones — no special equipment required.

🚶

Step 02

Start your walk

Head outside, to a park, or onto your treadmill. Any walk of any length works.

🧠

Step 03

Respond to audio challenges

Answer cognitive prompts as you walk, strengthening the brain‑body coordination that protects your balance.

The Science

Not just a wellness app. Evidence‑backed training.

Cognitive‑motor interference is what happens when your brain manages physical movement and mental tasks simultaneously. Research shows that training this dual‑task ability strengthens the neural pathways responsible for balance, attention, and fall prevention.

30+

Randomized controlled trials reviewed

76.6%

Of participants improved balance AND cognitive performance

55+

Years — the age group that benefits most

“A 2022 review of 30 randomized controlled trials found that 76.6% of participants in dual‑task training showed improved balance and cognitive performance.”

Int. J. Environ. Res. Public Health, 2022, 19, 16890

Who It's For

Built for active aging

🧓

Adults 55+

Looking to stay sharp and active as you age? StrideMind makes cognitive training a natural part of your daily walk — no gym, no screen time, no stress.

⚖️

Concerned About Falls

Falls are the leading cause of injury in older adults. Dual‑task walking has been clinically shown to improve balance and measurably reduce fall risk.

🏥

Physical Therapy Patients

StrideMind complements physical therapy by building cognitive‑motor coordination — the brain‑body link that protects you during everyday movement.

Testimonials

What people are saying

“I've been using StrideMind for three months and my balance has noticeably improved. I feel so much more confident on my morning walks.”

Margaret T.

Age 68

“My physical therapist recommended it after my fall last year. Now it's part of my daily routine — and I actually enjoy it.”

Robert K.

Age 72

“Simple to use, and the science actually checks out. My doctor was genuinely impressed when I showed her the research behind it.”

The first step toward sharper thinking and better balance starts with a walk. No gym. No equipment. Just you and your earbuds.
